article_meta.title: Marchers protesting planned deportations block major freeway in Los Angeles 1 of 18 | Thousands of people protesting mas

reasoning: The summary remains largely neutral, mirroring AP’s straightforward reporting without adopting loaded framing. Minor interpretive phrasing appears (e.g., “organized resistance,” “to draw attention to their cause”), but it does not meaningfully shift tone or insert subjective judgments.

custom_reasoning.spin: Mostly neutral verbs; slight interpretive phrasing like “organized resistance” and “disrupting major infrastructure.”
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All key details are grounded in the article (locations, actions, dates).
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: No subjective judgments presented as fact.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Language is measured; “major infrastructure” is mildly elevating but still factual.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: One inferred motive (“to draw attention to their cause”) introduces mild mind reading.
custom_reasoning.slant: No unequal emphasis beyond what the article provides.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Captures both protest actions and law enforcement presence as described.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balance matches the source’s factual depiction of protests and disruptions.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No preferential treatment of official vs. grassroots perspectives.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than any potential partisan framing; sticks to AP’s factual tone.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Reflects the single-source reporting and photo documentation included.
